title: trifolium longipes subsp. shastense
intro: Trifolium longipes subsp. shastense is a subspecies of the longstalk clover plant, native to the Klamath Mountains of northern California and southwestern Oregon in the United States. The medicinal properties of Trifolium longipes subsp. shastense include its ability to lower blood pressure, reduce inflammation, and act as an antioxidant, which are attributed to its high levels of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of menopausal hot flashes, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. Trifolium longipes subsp. shastense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its medicinal and health benefits.
